convention: art/generated/<category>/<id>.png is picked up automatically when the file exists; an explicit entry below overrides the convention path. Missing images are never an error - rendering falls back to the generated colors/shapes.
variant_convention: FQ-09V: consecutive files <id>_01.png, <id>_02.png, ... (max 8; the first gap ends the scan) form an optional variant pool, or an explicit entry below may be an array of paths. Blocks pick one variant deterministically from world seed + cell position; the choice never enters saves. A single <id>.png (or no file at all) keeps working exactly as before.
background_convention: FQ-09W: scenic backdrop layers (art/generated/backgrounds/) - surface_sky.png is a 640x360 full frame, surface_far_terrain.png and surface_mid_silhouette.png are horizontally tiling strips with transparency; consumed by scripts/world/world_backdrop.gd with stepped parallax, falling back to the code-drawn gradient/silhouettes. Backing-wall tiles (art/generated/back_walls/) are seamless opaque 16x16 (dirt_wall.png, stone_wall.png live; others per art/source_templates/BACKGROUND_TEMPLATE.md are planned); consumed by world._make_wall_texture, falling back to darkened block textures. All optional - missing files are never an error.
opening_convention: FQ-09C: the opening cinematic is code-plotted at 640x360 (scripts/shell/prologue_canvas.gd + prologue_puppets.gd) and integer-scaled 2x nearest into the 1280x720 viewport. Optional cel-shot upgrade per scene: a frame pool for a scene id (the variant convention opening_01_first_star_01.png ... or an explicit array entry here) plays those authored 640x360 frames at 8 fps in place of the plotted shot; an empty/removed pool always falls back to the code-drawn rendering. Scene ids follow docs/OPENING_STORYBOARD.md (opening_01_first_star ... opening_08_title_card). No frame may contain baked-in words - COHERONIA, By Paul Peck, the tagline, and all overlay copy are engine-rendered.
